name: Source Fidelity review for Ampk And Metabolic Modulators bundle
summary: The staged source, study, intervention, finding, and outlook records stay within the cited PubMed and ClinicalTrials.gov boundaries. PubMed titles, PMIDs, DOI links, trial registry status, enrollment counts, posted-result status, populations, endpoint classes, and key quantitative claims match the cited records closely enough for publication review; the important boundaries are already preserved as animal or non-human-primate support, small human biomarker evidence, null or mixed functional evidence, and results-pending registry activity.

findings.claim_or_issue: The PubMed title for PMID 40147475 includes a typographic spacing character before the MET-PREVENT parenthetical, while the staged source stores the same title with a normal ASCII space.
findings.why_it_matters: The source title should remain a reliable bibliographic anchor, but typography normalization should not block publication when the PMID, DOI, venue, authors, and substantive title all match.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the ASCII-normalized title unless the project later decides to preserve PubMed typography exactly.

findings.claim_or_issue: NCT02570672 has posted ClinicalTrials.gov results, and the staged finding correctly reports the frailty-index and Fried frailty score monthly-rate values without treating them as a clear functional benefit.
findings.why_it_matters: Registry-posted outcomes can support a boundary finding, but they should not be inflated into a positive frailty-prevention claim without a peer-reviewed outcome interpretation and statistical context.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the mixed direction, low confidence, and registry-result caveats.

findings.claim_or_issue: NCT04264897 is completed and has no posted results in the ClinicalTrials.gov record checked for this review.
findings.why_it_matters: A completed trial is a watch item and momentum signal, but without posted or published results it cannot support benefit, harm, or a rating upgrade.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the regulatory_or_program_update evidence tier, inconclusive direction, and no-results caveat.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 29383869 supports older-adult metabolic and transcriptomic biomarker claims, including 14 participants, six-week treatment periods, 647 muscle genes, and 146 adipose genes, but not functional or clinical benefit.
findings.why_it_matters: This source is the main human anchor for the proposed stage and must stay in the biomarker lane.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; the staged finding and outlook keep this result at human_biomarker_signal and include small-sample and endpoint caveats.
